description: Add multi-instance support for plugins with url-type config fields. When a url-type config value is a Record<string, string> (name→URL map), the platform derives match patterns for all URLs, injects instance-specific config per tab, adds an 'instance' parameter to tool schemas, and resolves instance names to tabs at dispatch time. Enables plugins like SQLPad to work with both production and staging simultaneously without any plugin code changes.

userStories.title: Change url config value type to Record<string, string>
userStories.description: Change the url-type config field value from a plain string to always be a Record<string, string> (instance name → URL map). Update the shared types, manifest validation, and all config reading/writing code.
acceptanceCriteria: url-type config values are stored as Record<string, string> in config.json
Manifest validation (parsePluginPackageJson) still allows empty urlPatterns when configSchema has a required url field
The ConfigSettingDefinition type is unchanged — the value shape change is in config.json storage, not the schema definition
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts (config read/write for settings)
- platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts (POST /plugin-settings validation)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (config.setPluginSettings handler)

Context:
Currently url-type config values are stored as plain strings in config.json:
```json
{ "settings": { "sqlpad": { "instanceUrl": "https://sqlpad.production.brexapps.io" } } }
```

The new format uses a named map:
```json
{ "settings": { "sqlpad": { "instanceUrl": { "production": "https://sqlpad.production.brexapps.io" } } } }
```

Changes:
1. In http-routes.ts handlePluginSettings (line 447): the validation currently just checks `typeof body.settings === 'object'`. It passes the raw object through to `state.pluginSettings` and `savePluginSettings`. The url field values inside settings should now be validated as `Record<string, string>` when the matching configSchema field has `type: 'url'`. Find the registered plugin from `state.registry.plugins` by name, read its `configSchema`, and for each url-type field, validate the value is a non-empty `Record<string, string>` where every key is a non-empty string and every value is a valid URL string.

2. In config.ts savePluginSettings: no changes needed — it already writes arbitrary JSON.

3. In extension-handlers.ts handleConfigSetPluginSettings: no changes needed — it passes through to savePluginSettings.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change ConfigSettingDefinition or ConfigSchema types — those describe the schema, not the stored values
- Do NOT change the config.json file format for non-url fields (string, number, boolean, select remain unchanged)
- The http-routes validation should reject the old string format for url fields — no backwards compat

userStories.title: Update settings resolver for multi-instance URL patterns
userStories.description: Update resolvePluginSettings to handle Record<string, string> url config values. Derive a match pattern for each URL in the map. Return an instanceMap mapping instance names to their URL patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: resolvePluginSettings derives one match pattern per URL in the Record<string, string> map
ResolvedSettings includes an instanceMap field: Record<string, string> mapping instance name to derived match pattern
resolvedValues for url fields stores the full Record<string, string> map
effectiveHomepage is derived from the first URL in the map
Invalid URLs within the map are skipped with a warning

userStories.title: Store instanceMap on RegisteredPlugin and propagate to extension
userStories.description: Add instanceMap to RegisteredPlugin in state.ts and include it in the ConfigStatePlugin payload sent to the extension. Update the discovery pipeline to pass instanceMap from resolved settings to the registered plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: RegisteredPlugin has an instanceMap field: Record<string, string> (instance name → match pattern)
ConfigStatePlugin in shared/src/index.ts includes instanceMap
buildConfigStatePayload includes instanceMap in each plugin's serialized state
The extension receives instanceMap via sync.full and plugins.changed messages

userStories.title: Inject instance parameter into plugin tool schemas
userStories.description: In mcp-setup.ts getAllToolsList, inject an 'instance' enum parameter into plugin tool schemas when the plugin has an instanceMap with 2+ entries. Make it required. Do not inject it for single-instance or no-instance plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin tools with instanceMap.size >= 2 have a required 'instance' string enum parameter in their schema
The enum values are the instance names from instanceMap (e.g., ['production', 'staging'])
Plugin tools with 0-1 instances do NOT have an 'instance' parameter
The 'instance' parameter description explains what it does
tabId parameter is still injected alongside instance

userStories.title: Resolve instance to tab in dispatch pipeline
userStories.description: In mcp-tool-dispatch.ts, extract the 'instance' parameter from tool args before Ajv validation (alongside tabId extraction). Resolve instance name → match pattern → matching tab. Use the resolved tab for dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'instance' parameter is stripped from args before Ajv validation
When instance is provided, the dispatch finds the tab whose URL matches the instance's pattern
When instance is provided but no matching tab is open, an error is returned describing which instance tab is needed
When both instance and tabId are provided, instance takes precedence
When neither is provided for a multi-instance plugin, auto-select works as before

userStories.title: Add instance labels to plugin_list_tabs output
userStories.description: Enhance plugin_list_tabs to include an 'instance' field on each tab entry, derived by matching the tab's URL against the plugin's instanceMap.
acceptanceCriteria: Each tab in plugin_list_tabs output includes an 'instance' string field when the plugin has an instanceMap
The instance value is the name of the matching instance (e.g., 'production', 'staging')
Tabs that don't match any instance pattern have instance: undefined
The output still includes tabId, url, title, ready, and connectionId

userStories.title: Inject per-instance config into tabs
userStories.description: Update the extension's config injection to resolve which instance a tab belongs to and inject only that instance's URL value (not the full map) into pluginConfig. This way getConfig('instanceUrl') returns the correct URL string for the current tab's instance.
acceptanceCriteria: When a plugin has instanceMap, getConfig('instanceUrl') in a production tab returns the production URL string
When a plugin has instanceMap, getConfig('instanceUrl') in a staging tab returns the staging URL string
Non-url config fields are injected identically to all tabs
Plugins without instanceMap continue to work unchanged

userStories.title: Update ConfigDialog UI for named URL instances
userStories.description: Update the side panel ConfigDialog to render url-type fields as a list of named instances with add/remove functionality, fitting the retro design system.
acceptanceCriteria: url-type fields render as a list of instance entries, each with a name input and URL input on the same row
An 'Add instance' button appends a new empty row
Each instance row has a remove button (except when only 1 remains if required)
Validation ensures all instance names are non-empty and unique, all URLs are valid
The saved value is a Record<string, string> map
All UI elements use retro theme tokens (border-2 border-border rounded shadow-sm font-mono bg-card text-foreground)
The add/remove buttons match the retro style
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfigDialog.tsx

Context:
The current url field rendering (ConfigDialog.tsx lines 144-153):
```tsx
<Input
  id={`config-${key}`}
  name={key}
  type={def.type === 'number' ? 'number' : 'text'}
  placeholder={def.placeholder ?? ''}
  defaultValue={resolvedSettings?.[key] != null ? String(resolvedSettings[key]) : ''}
  aria-invalid={Boolean(errors[key])}
  className="py-1.5 text-sm"
/>
```

Changes:
For url-type fields, replace the single Input with an InstanceUrlField component. This component manages a list of name→URL entries as local state.

The UI design for each instance row (fits retro theme):
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SQLPad URL *                                     │
│ The URL of your SQLPad instance                  │
│                                                  │
│ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────┐ ╳ │
│ │production│ │https://sqlpad.prod.brex.io   │   │
│ └──────────┘ └──────────────────────────────┘   │
│ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────────┐ ╳ │
│ │staging   │ │https://sqlpad.staging.brex.io│   │
│ └──────────┘ └──────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                  │
│ [+ Add instance]                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The name input is ~30% width, URL input is ~60% width, remove button is the remaining.

Since ConfigDialog uses an uncontrolled form pattern with formRef, the url instance list needs to be controlled state within the dialog. Use useState to manage the entries array: `Array<{ name: string; url: string }>`. Initialize from resolvedSettings on mount.

For `handleSave`, when the field type is 'url', read from the controlled state instead of form elements. Build the Record<string, string> from the entries array.

For defaultValue initialization, the resolvedSettings for a url field is the full Record<string, string> map (from US-003 ConfigStatePlugin). Parse it into the entries array.

Wait — actually, resolvedSettings for url fields will be the per-tab resolved string (from US-007). But in the ConfigDialog, we want to show all instances. The dialog needs access to the full map. Two options:
1. The ConfigDialog reads from a different source (the raw config, not resolvedSettings)
2. The ConfigStatePlugin includes both resolvedSettings (per-tab) and the raw config values

The simplest approach: add a `rawSettings` field to ConfigStatePlugin that contains the unresolved config values. Or, since the ConfigDialog is about editing settings, it can read from the raw config. Actually, looking at the current code, `resolvedSettings` is what's passed to ConfigDialog. The simplest fix: pass the raw settings separately. Add a `configValues` field to ConfigStatePlugin containing the raw values from config.json (not the per-tab resolved values). The ConfigDialog reads from `configValues` for initialization and writes back the same shape.

Alternatively, keep things simple: include instanceMap in the ConfigStatePlugin and reconstruct the url map from instanceMap (pattern → name) combined with the actual URLs. But this is fragile.

Best approach: add `rawConfigValues?: Record<string, unknown>` to ConfigStatePlugin. In buildConfigStatePayload, populate it from state.pluginSettings[pluginName]. The ConfigDialog uses rawConfigValues for initialization (not resolvedSettings). This cleanly separates "what the tab sees" (resolvedSettings) from "what the user configured" (rawConfigValues).

Retro style requirements:
- All inputs use the existing retro Input component
- Remove button: use a small retro Button with variant='outline' and an X icon, or a text button with 'text-destructive'
- Add button: retro Button variant='outline' size='sm' with '+ Add instance' label
- Each row is a flex container with gap-2
- Instance name input has placeholder 'Name (e.g. production)'
- URL input has the configSchema's placeholder

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the retro component primitives
- The add/remove interaction must be smooth — no jarring re-renders
- At least one entry must remain if the field is required

userStories.title: Update CLI plugin configure for named URL instances
userStories.description: Update the CLI handlePluginConfigure and promptField to support entering named URL instances interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: For url-type fields, the CLI prompts for instance name + URL pairs
Users can add multiple instances by typing 'y' when asked 'Add another instance?'
The saved value is a Record<string, string> map
Current values are shown as defaults when re-configuring
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts (promptField function, around line 780; handlePluginConfigure, around line 881)

Context:
The current url field prompting in promptField (lines 856-879):
```ts
// url or string
const hint = hasCurrentValue ? String(currentValue) : (definition.placeholder ?? '');
const prompt = hint ? `  Value [${hint}]: ` : '  Value: ';
while (true) {
  const answer = await rl.question(prompt);
  ...
  if (definition.type === 'url') {
    try { new URL(answer.trim()); } catch { ... }
  }
  return answer.trim();
}
```

Changes:
For url-type fields, replace the simple prompt with a multi-instance flow:

```
  SQLPad URL *
  The URL of your SQLPad instance

  Instance 1:
    Name [production]: 
    URL [https://sqlpad.prod.brex.io]: 
  Add another instance? (y/n) [n]: y
  Instance 2:
    Name: staging
    URL: https://sqlpad.staging.brex.io
  Add another instance? (y/n) [n]: n
```

Return value should be `Record<string, string>` for url fields instead of a single string.

The function signature of promptField returns `Promise<string | number | boolean | undefined>`. This needs to change to `Promise<string | number | boolean | Record<string, string> | undefined>` to support the url map return type.

For re-configuration, if currentValue is a Record<string, string>, show existing instances as defaults.

Constraints:
- Non-url field prompting is unchanged
- The flow must handle the case where the user enters only one instance (still stored as Record with one entry)

userStories.title: Add unit tests for multi-instance dispatch and schema injection
userStories.description: Add comprehensive unit tests for the instance parameter injection in mcp-setup.ts and the instance-to-tab resolution in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests verify instance enum is injected into tool schemas when instanceMap has 2+ entries
Tests verify instance is NOT injected when instanceMap has 0-1 entries
Tests verify instance is required in the schema
Tests verify instance is stripped from args before Ajv validation
Tests verify instance resolves to the correct tab
Tests verify error when instance tab is not open
Tests verify instance takes precedence over tabId
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts (add tests for getAllToolsList instance injection)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts (add tests for instance extraction and resolution)

Context:
Existing tests in mcp-setup.test.ts test getAllToolsList for tabId injection and permission prefixes. Follow the same patterns.

For dispatch tests, mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts has extensive test helpers for creating mock state, plugins, and tab mappings. Use the existing test infrastructure.

Test cases for mcp-setup.test.ts:
1. Plugin with instanceMap { production: '*://prod.example.com/*', staging: '*://staging.example.com/*' } → tools have `instance` param with enum ['production', 'staging'] and it's required
2. Plugin with instanceMap { default: '*://app.example.com/*' } → tools do NOT have `instance` param (only 1 instance)
3. Plugin with no instanceMap → tools do NOT have `instance` param
4. Instance enum values match the instanceMap keys exactly

Test cases for mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts:
1. Tool called with `instance: 'staging'` → dispatched to the staging tab
2. Tool called with `instance: 'staging'` but staging tab not open → error returned
3. Tool called with `instance: 'nonexistent'` → error listing valid instances
4. Tool called with both `instance` and `tabId` → instance takes precedence
5. Tool called without `instance` on multi-instance plugin → auto-select works
6. `instance` is stripped from args before Ajv validation

Constraints:
- Use existing test helpers and mock factories — do NOT create new test infrastructure

userStories.title: Add E2E test for multi-instance plugin dispatch
userStories.description: Add an E2E test that verifies the complete multi-instance flow: configure multiple URLs, verify both tabs are matched, dispatch to a specific instance, and verify instance labels in plugin_list_tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test creates a plugin with configSchema url field
Test configures two instances via config.json
Test verifies both instance tabs appear in plugin_list_tabs with correct instance labels
Test verifies dispatching with instance parameter hits the correct tab
Test verifies dispatching with wrong instance returns an error
Test verifies getConfig returns the per-tab URL in each instance
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/multi-instance.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
Existing E2E tests in e2e/ use the test infrastructure from e2e/fixtures.ts and e2e/helpers.ts. The test-server.ts creates a local HTTP server for testing. Look at e2e/plugin-settings.e2e.ts and e2e/multi-tab-targeting.e2e.ts for patterns.

The test should:
1. Create a test plugin with `configSchema: { instanceUrl: { type: 'url', label: 'URL', required: true } }` and empty urlPatterns
2. Write config.json with `settings: { testplugin: { instanceUrl: { alpha: 'http://localhost:PORT1', beta: 'http://localhost:PORT2' } } }`
3. Start two local test servers on different ports
4. Start the MCP server, connect extension
5. Open tabs to both localhost:PORT1 and localhost:PORT2
6. Call plugin_list_tabs and verify both tabs appear with instance labels 'alpha' and 'beta'
7. Call the test plugin's tool with `instance: 'alpha'` and verify it dispatches to PORT1
8. Call the test plugin's tool with `instance: 'beta'` and verify it dispatches to PORT2
9. Call with `instance: 'gamma'` and verify the error message
10. Verify getConfig('instanceUrl') returns the correct URL in each tab

Follow the existing test patterns for creating test plugins, managing config, and dispatching tools.

Constraints:
- Use the existing test infrastructure (fixtures.ts, helpers.ts, test-server.ts)
- Clean up test servers and temp files in afterAll
- The test must be self-contained and not depend on external services
